name: customer-io
description: Search Customer.io people, inspect campaigns, segments, and metrics, and send transactional email

envVars.name: CUSTOMERIO_APP_API_KEY
envVars.description: Customer.io App API Key (Bearer token for api.customer.io)

tools.name: Search Customers
tools.description: Search Customer.io people by attribute or segment filter conditions

start.description: Pagination token from the previous page's next field

tools.name: List Campaigns
tools.description: List campaigns in the Customer.io workspace with their state and goals

tools.name: Get Campaign Metrics
tools.description: Get delivery and conversion metrics for a Customer.io campaign over a time period

version.description: Version of the metrics API to use: "1" or "2" (required; the period and steps parameters only apply to version 1)

period.description: Bucket size for the series: hours, days, weeks, or months (version 1 only)

steps.description: Number of periods to return (version 1 only; defaults to the maximum available, or 12 if the period is in months; maximums are 24 hours, 45 days, 12 weeks, or 120 months)

type.description: Restrict metrics to a message type: email, webhook, twilio, whatsapp, slack, push, or in_app

tools.name: Get Person Attributes
tools.description: Get a person's full attribute profile by ID, email, or cio_id (Search Customers returns only identifiers)

customerId.description: The person's identifier - an id, email address, or cio_id depending on id_type

id_type.description: Type of identifier in customerId: id (default), email, or cio_id

tools.name: List Transactional Messages
tools.description: List the workspace's transactional message templates so agents can find a valid transactional_message_id before sending

tools.name: Trigger Broadcast
tools.description: Trigger an API-triggered broadcast campaign, optionally overriding the recipients and passing liquid data into the messages

broadcastId.description: ID of the API-triggered broadcast to trigger (from List Campaigns; the campaign must be of the API-triggered broadcast type)

data.description: Key-value data merged into the broadcast messages via liquid, e.g. {"headline": "..."}

recipients.description: Optional audience filter overriding the broadcast's default recipients, e.g. {"segment": {"id": 7}} or and/or/not condition arrays

emails.description: Optional array of existing people's email addresses to send the broadcast to (alternative to recipients)

ids.description: Optional array of existing people's IDs to send the broadcast to (alternative to recipients)

email_ignore_missing.description: If true, skip missing email addresses instead of failing the broadcast

id_ignore_missing.description: If true, skip missing person IDs instead of failing the broadcast

tools.name: List Segments
tools.description: List segments in the Customer.io workspace so agents can target or filter people

tools.name: Send Transactional Email
tools.description: Send a transactional email to a recipient, using a transactional message template or inline subject and body

identifiers.description: Person the message belongs to: one of, and only one of, {"id":"42"}, {"email":"user@example.com"}, or {"cio_id":"..."} (optional, e.g. for non-person sends)

transactional_message_id.description: ID or trigger name of the transactional message template (omit when sending inline subject and body)

message_data.description: Key-value data merged into the message template via liquid

subject.description: Inline subject line (when not using a transactional message template)

body.description: Inline HTML body (when not using a transactional message template)

prompts.title: Campaign report
prompts.prompt: List my Customer.io campaigns and summarize the recent metrics for the active ones.

prompts.title: Find a person
prompts.prompt: Search Customer.io for a person by email and show their identifiers.

prompts.title: Send a transactional email
prompts.prompt: Send a transactional email via Customer.io to the recipient I specify.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Customer.io setup

steps.title: Create a Customer.io account
steps.description: Sign up at https://customer.io/ — the free trial includes a full workspace suitable for development and testing.

steps.title: Create an App API key
steps.description: In Customer.io go to Settings > Account Settings > API Credentials, open the App API Keys tab, and create a key. Scope it to the workspace and permissions your agents need (read access plus transactional send if you use the email tool).

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Copy the key into CUSTOMERIO_APP_API_KEY. It is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header on every request.

steps.title: Optional: set up a transactional message
steps.description: To send templated transactional email, create a Transactional message in Customer.io and use its ID or trigger name as transactional_message_id.

notes: Customer.io has US and EU regions. This connector targets the US host api.customer.io; if your workspace is in the EU data center, replace the host with api-eu.customer.io in the tool URLs.

These tools use the App API (Bearer App API key). The separate Track API (track.customer.io) for identifying people and sending events uses HTTP Basic auth with a site ID and Track API key and is not covered by this connector.
App API rate limits: most endpoints allow 10 requests per second; transactional sends allow 100 requests per second.
